reviews_data.review_text:
I came in today around 12:00 to pick up four items in person—three bowls and a toast. I understand that everything is made to order; however, being pregnant, I am very mindful of wait times and how long food takes.

We waited about 20 minutes. My husband and son were inside while I was mostly outside. During that time, I noticed two other customers who arrived about 10 minutes after us were served before we were. There didn’t appear to be a large rush or many other orders ahead of us.

After waiting, I went inside and asked my husband what was going on. He told me they were “almost done with the bowls.” A few moments later, we were handed the bowls in a bag and told that everything is made fresh to order. I found that comment a bit unnecessary, especially since it was clear we were already waiting longer than expected.

At that point, my husband realized the toast we ordered had been missed. The staff then said they would make it now. By then, it had already been about 25 minutes total, and we decided we no longer wanted it due to the extended wait, the missed item, and the fact that other orders placed after ours were completed first.

Even though we had already paid, and the toast was eventually brought out after we declined it, we did not take it because it had already been about 30 minutes total, and we were ready to leave.

reviews_data.review_text: I randomly googled “juice bars near me” as I was craving a smoothie while at work. Toastique was the closest so I picked it. I didn’t have any expectations as I just wanted a smoothie & was unable to make mine for work that day. When I got off from work, my friend & I met up there. Upon entry we were greeted by two lovely ladies & a gentlemen (James). Soft music was playing, counter tops and tables were very clean, the establishment itself smelt fresh and there were a few people who looked to be enjoying their purchases. The vibe was very relaxing & welcoming.  Looking at the great options on the menu, I decided to go with a smoothie bowl called “DragonBerry” instead of a smoothie. My friend ordered a “PB+B” bowl. I was so glad I went with my choice. It was so delicious I didn’t get a chance to take a picture. I usually don’t go for smoothie bowls because most places give you such a small amount of the base then throw loads of toppings on and you are just eating toppings at that point for an insane price. Toastique’s bowls were PERFECTLY proportioned toppings and base wise. The flavor was amazing and I was full when I finished. It was also a great price for the amount of product we received. I was shocked. The bathroom was also VERY clean as that’s something my friend is very funny about. As he says “why would I want to eat some where that the people making my food also use and it’s dirty?” Haha. James also offered us samples of each fresh pressed juice.  We ended up taking them to go and they were also VERY delicious. Everyone who knows me knows I DESPISE carrots & I CHUGGED the “Defender” fresh pressed juice. If you have a chance and you’re in the area DEFINITELY check Toastique’s out! You won’t be disappointed. Customer service and food 10 out of 10! I can’t wait to go back.

reviews_data.review_text:
Let me just say — this was easily one of the best breakfast I’ve had in a while.

First, the sanitation and cleanliness stood out immediately. The space was spotless, organized, and had that fresh, crisp feel you want in a café that focuses on high-quality ingredients. You can tell they care about presentation and detail.

Speaking of presentation… the food looked SO appetizing. Everything was vibrant, colorful, and plated beautifully. It honestly looked like something straight off Pinterest — but better because it was real and right in front of me.

Now let’s talk taste.

I tried:
•	Smoked Salmon Toast
•	Spicy Crab Toast

Both were absolutely delicious. The smoked salmon was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly balanced. The spicy crab had just the right kick — not overpowering, but enough heat to make every bite exciting. The textures were layered so well: creamy, crunchy, savory, and slightly spicy all at once.

It’s rare when something looks amazing and tastes even better — but Toastique delivered on both.

If you’re looking for a fresh, elevated breakfast or lunch option that feels healthy but still satisfying, this is it. 10/10 would order again.

reviews_data.review_text:
Overall: 7/10 I mean it’s toast and juice in uptown, of course it’s overpriced, but it’s cute, and the people are friendly and service is relatively quick.

Decor/Vibes: 10/10 super cute spot, trendy location. Plenty of natural light, upbeat music, open concept.

Customer service: 10/10 super nice people, friendly, helps you navigate the menu.

Food: 5/10 Flavor wise it’s good. Flavorful, fresh, not revolutionary, but it’s tasty. I’m full without feeling stuffed or bloated. Playing. And presentation is also very gorgeous. Here’s my main issue. The toast looks beautiful but is almost impossible to actually eat. Items are cut way too large. The bread is a good thickness but not toasty enough to handle the toppings without turning to mush. If I’m paying $15 for toast I want a crispy bread and all of my toppings in every bite. We tried 2 different toasts and they were tasty but ridiculously messy even with a fork and knife.

Since this is in uptown I’m assuming they’re targeting people on their way to work or stopping for lunch, I don’t think the toast would travel well. Again, we only tried the toast - maybe the other items are better. And let’s be real, most restaurants in this area have similar quality.

I came in today around 12:00 to pick up four items in person—three bowls and a toast. I understand that everything is made to order; however, being pregnant, I am very mindful of wait times and how long food takes.

We waited about 20 minutes. My husband and son were inside while I was mostly outside. During that time, I noticed two other customers who arrived about 10 minutes after us were served before we were. There didn’t appear to be a large rush or many other orders ahead of us.

After waiting, I went inside and asked my husband what was going on. He told me they were “almost done with the bowls.” A few moments later, we were handed the bowls in a bag and told that everything is made fresh to order. I found that comment a bit unnecessary, especially since it was clear we were already waiting longer than expected.

At that point, my husband realized the toast we ordered had been missed. The staff then said they would make it now. By then, it had already been about 25 minutes total, and we decided we no longer wanted it due to the extended wait, the missed item, and the fact that other orders placed after ours were completed first.

Even though we had already paid, and the toast was eventually brought out after we declined it, we did not take it because it had already been about 30 minutes total, and we were ready to leave.

reviews_data.review_text: I randomly googled “juice bars near me” as I was craving a smoothie while at work. Toastique was the closest so I picked it. I didn’t have any expectations as I just wanted a smoothie & was unable to make mine for work that day. When I got off from work, my friend & I met up there. Upon entry we were greeted by two lovely ladies & a gentlemen (James). Soft music was playing, counter tops and tables were very clean, the establishment itself smelt fresh and there were a few people who looked to be enjoying their purchases. The vibe was very relaxing & welcoming.  Looking at the great options on the menu, I decided to go with a smoothie bowl called “DragonBerry” instead of a smoothie. My friend ordered a “PB+B” bowl. I was so glad I went with my choice. It was so delicious I didn’t get a chance to take a picture. I usually don’t go for smoothie bowls because most places give you such a small amount of the base then throw loads of toppings on and you are just eating toppings at that point for an insane price. Toastique’s bowls were PERFECTLY proportioned toppings and base wise. The flavor was amazing and I was full when I finished. It was also a great price for the amount of product we received. I was shocked. The bathroom was also VERY clean as that’s something my friend is very funny about. As he says “why would I want to eat some where that the people making my food also use and it’s dirty?” Haha. James also offered us samples of each fresh pressed juice.  We ended up taking them to go and they were also VERY delicious. Everyone who knows me knows I DESPISE carrots & I CHUGGED the “Defender” fresh pressed juice. If you have a chance and you’re in the area DEFINITELY check Toastique’s out! You won’t be disappointed. Customer service and food 10 out of 10! I can’t wait to go back.

reviews_data.review_text:
Let me just say — this was easily one of the best breakfast I’ve had in a while.

First, the sanitation and cleanliness stood out immediately. The space was spotless, organized, and had that fresh, crisp feel you want in a café that focuses on high-quality ingredients. You can tell they care about presentation and detail.

Speaking of presentation… the food looked SO appetizing. Everything was vibrant, colorful, and plated beautifully. It honestly looked like something straight off Pinterest — but better because it was real and right in front of me.

Now let’s talk taste.

I tried:
•	Smoked Salmon Toast
•	Spicy Crab Toast

Both were absolutely delicious. The smoked salmon was fresh, flavorful, and perfectly balanced. The spicy crab had just the right kick — not overpowering, but enough heat to make every bite exciting. The textures were layered so well: creamy, crunchy, savory, and slightly spicy all at once.

It’s rare when something looks amazing and tastes even better — but Toastique delivered on both.

If you’re looking for a fresh, elevated breakfast or lunch option that feels healthy but still satisfying, this is it. 10/10 would order again.

Overall: 7/10 I mean it’s toast and juice in uptown, of course it’s overpriced, but it’s cute, and the people are friendly and service is relatively quick.

Decor/Vibes: 10/10 super cute spot, trendy location. Plenty of natural light, upbeat music, open concept.

Customer service: 10/10 super nice people, friendly, helps you navigate the menu.

Food: 5/10 Flavor wise it’s good. Flavorful, fresh, not revolutionary, but it’s tasty. I’m full without feeling stuffed or bloated. Playing. And presentation is also very gorgeous. Here’s my main issue. The toast looks beautiful but is almost impossible to actually eat. Items are cut way too large. The bread is a good thickness but not toasty enough to handle the toppings without turning to mush. If I’m paying $15 for toast I want a crispy bread and all of my toppings in every bite. We tried 2 different toasts and they were tasty but ridiculously messy even with a fork and knife.

Since this is in uptown I’m assuming they’re targeting people on their way to work or stopping for lunch, I don’t think the toast would travel well. Again, we only tried the toast - maybe the other items are better. And let’s be real, most restaurants in this area have similar quality.
